currentPath.summary: Discovery-first product and business build: turn planning into publishable site structure, clearer command surfaces, and a working launch flow while keeping autonomous activity visible.
focus: Turn founder answers into durable planning docs.
Translate planning into public-facing messaging and page structure.
Make the dashboard a clearer command surface instead of a long scrolling log.
Keep heartbeat/autonomous activity visible enough that Kirill can tell what is running and why.

trackStatus.name: Discovery / founder signal
trackStatus.summary: Trista has already replied with the strongest founder answers so far, and that material is currently being turned into launch sequencing and content decisions.
trackStatus.lastAction: Communicated with Trista by email; she replied with audience, launch-channel, product, urgency, and long-term direction details, which were distilled into planning docs and a launch sequencing recommendation.
trackStatus.assessment: I am not currently blocked on Trista for the present step. I already have enough signal to keep shaping the first release, and any next outreach should be narrower and tactical rather than broad discovery. When communicating with her, the default should now be replying in the known working thread instead of fresh compose.
trackStatus.nextMove: Use the first-content-topics shortlist to decide whether the first public release should expose one dancer topic and one parent topic immediately or show the lanes first with placeholders, and use the known working Trista thread for the next tactical follow-up.
trackStatus.dependency: Not currently waiting on Trista for the current step.

trackStatus.name: Systems / prototype build
trackStatus.summary: Both primary audience lanes now have a first real long-form article draft locally, so the site is starting to become actual guidance instead of mostly placeholders.
trackStatus.lastAction: Drafted /blog/how-parents-can-support-a-serious-young-dancer-without-over-managing.html and linked it from the parent guidance lane locally.
trackStatus.assessment: The content lanes are now deep enough to justify a publish pass. The next useful move is bundling the refreshed dashboard feed with both new article pages and their updated lane hubs so Kirill has something materially new to review.
trackStatus.nextMove: Publish the two first-article pages, the refreshed lane hubs, and the updated dashboard feed to GitHub Pages, then notify Kirill with the links.

trackStatus.name: Dashboard / visibility / reporting
trackStatus.summary: The dashboard is being reworked into a more honest command surface: blockers get their own tab, Start Here should be directly visible, and public sync state should stop being implicit.
trackStatus.lastAction: Updated the local dashboard structure to add a dedicated blockers tab, a visible Start Here link, and an explicit publish/QA state section.
trackStatus.assessment: The main visibility failure was not lack of work but lack of explicit public-sync truthfulness. The dashboard now needs a republish plus public QA so Kirill can trust it again.
trackStatus.nextMove: Publish the updated dashboard, dashboard-data feed, refreshed lane pages, and first article pages; then verify the real public URLs and feed timestamps before calling it done.

nextSteps.title: Republish dashboard + article pages + lane hubs with strict public QA
nextSteps.why: The dashboard and page work is no longer enough locally; the current highest-value move is to get public state caught up and verified so 404s or stale feed drift cannot hide again.

nextSteps.title: Keep blockers in a dedicated dashboard tab and make Start Here visible from the command surface
nextSteps.why: Kirill asked for blocker visibility without scrolling and for Start Here to be reachable directly from the dashboard.

nextSteps.title: Treat Trista outbound email as unreliable until a better channel or Gmail fallback is ready
nextSteps.why: Trista reported not seeing the earlier attempted update, so Outlook UI state is not trustworthy as delivery proof right now.

completed: Created and published the first live GitHub Pages site at https://origin4124.github.io/.
Replaced the original proof page with a more real Thriving Dancer landing page.
Added a studio scheduling / management concept section to the public site.
Verified the GitHub Pages update after propagation and closed the publish loop.
Sent Telegram notification with the live URL once the site was ready.
Established direct email communication with Trista.

Captured a strong first signal set from Trista: credentials, performance history, teaching background, founder role, age 3–14 mission focus, growth mindset, developmentally appropriate training, and whole-person philosophy.

Captured clearer founder answers: first audience = pre-professional dancers ages 10–16, first launch = podcast + blog, early products = workbook + ballet unit study, and the studio software lane is both internal and potentially product-facing later.
Set up an autonomous Thriving Dancer assessment heartbeat to decide and take the next bounded project step.
Built and published a live Thriving Dancer project dashboard at /dashboard.html.
Updated the homepage and dashboard gates to minimal text only.

Created a working discovery brief, Planning Pack v1, website/content architecture draft, homepage copy draft, about-page outline, hero/CTA options, Start Here hub structure, first-visit flow draft, first public launch checklist, local Start Here prototype, and launch sequencing recommendation inside the project folder.
Published the homepage -> Start Here visitor path to GitHub Pages and notified Kirill with links.
Drafted the first real dancer-facing article, What pre-professional ballet training actually asks of you, and linked it from the dancer guidance lane locally.
Drafted the first real parent-facing article, How parents can support a serious young dancer without over-managing, and linked it from the parent guidance lane locally.

blockers.label: Public dashboard feed is behind local state
blockers.detail: Local dashboard state has advanced to include the first article drafts and newer heartbeat breadcrumbs, but the public dashboard feed is still behind and needs a clean republish plus verification. This remains active until the public dashboard-data.json timestamp and page behavior match the newer local state.

communication.summary: Confirmed directly that the public dashboard was still stale at 14:36 EDT while local state had advanced to 17:20 EDT, and explained that publish/verification was the lagging step.

blockers.label: Outlook reliability
blockers.detail: Outlook Web was flaky on the fresh-compose path earlier, but the resend through the existing known-working Trista thread completed cleanly around 12:39 PM EDT. This is no longer blocking the morning-report loop, but it remains a reliability risk to watch.

communication.summary: Sent a blocker alert about the failed fresh-compose Trista email path; later resolved through the known working thread.

blockers.label: Native channel runtime
blockers.detail: Native Telegram/Discord channel registration in OpenClaw is still broken, so alerts and messaging continue to depend on Telegram Bot API and browser-based workarounds.

communication.summary: Repeatedly communicated during the debugging track; native channels remained unavailable while Telegram direct fallback worked.

telegram.event: Thriving Dancer site became review-ready and complete.
Dashboard passphrase/project-log update finished locally and Outlook was cleaned down to one main tab.
Homepage proof-of-concept was updated to use the same light passphrase gate as the dashboard.
Published the tabbed dashboard, heartbeat/automation visibility, and minimal-text gates to GitHub Pages.
Published the homepage -> Start Here visitor path to GitHub Pages.
Published the three-lane Start Here scaffold to GitHub Pages.

notifications.reviewReadyPolicy: Ping Kirill on Telegram when review is ready and again when the full loop is complete for important operations.
notifications.taskCompletionPreference: Ping Kirill on Telegram every time a task is finished.
notifications.githubVisibilityRule: When GitHub-hosted pages change in a way that gives Kirill something new to see, send a concise message with the relevant link after the change is live or reasonably visible.

automation.summary: Current autonomous activity and heartbeat behavior that keeps the project and ops surfaces moving across discovery and systems/prototype tracks.

heartbeatQuality.standard: Each Thriving Dancer heartbeat should try to leave one meaningful bounded advance; pure status confirmation is a weak pass unless real blockers or comms/recovery work consumed the run.
heartbeatQuality.weakPassPolicy: If a pass only confirms status, treat it as weak and bias the next pass toward a stronger bounded move.
heartbeatQuality.visibilityExpectation: Heartbeat breadcrumbs should show what the run attempted and whether it was meaningful or weak.

automation.lastHeartbeatSummary: The 17:35 PM EDT heartbeat hardened the dashboard structure around blocker visibility and public-sync honesty instead of leaving blockers buried in the overview.

recentHeartbeats.result: Created `/blog/parents.html` and rewired the Start Here parent CTA toward that real placeholder destination locally.

recentHeartbeats.attempted: Create the first real destination page for the dancer lane and connect Start Here to it.
recentHeartbeats.result: Created `/blog/dancers.html` and rewired the Start Here dancer CTA toward that real placeholder destination locally.

recentHeartbeats.result: Confirmed the public dashboard-data feed is live and created `cta-destination-map-v1.md` to map homepage/Start Here CTAs into real next destination pages.

recentHeartbeats.result: Published the newer dashboard feed and renderer with 15-minute cadence visibility, blocker status, communicated-to-Kirill state, and newer heartbeat breadcrumbs.

recentHeartbeats.result: Resent the update in the existing Trista thread and verified a clean replied state instead of the earlier fresh-compose failure.
Wired the local homepage nav and primary CTA toward the real Start Here page.

recentHeartbeats.attempted: Check due work and current blocker state.
recentHeartbeats.result: Mostly confirmed the unresolved Trista email blocker and did not leave a strong project artifact; this is now treated as a weak pass example.

tabState.notes: Service workers, shared workers, and iframe noise are ignored; cleanup focuses on top-level page tabs.

items.name: Thriving Dancer project heartbeat
items.purpose: Assess project state, choose the most useful bounded next step, and make concrete progress.
currentBehavior: Reassess project state instead of following a hard-coded task list.
Prefer one bounded useful step per pass.
Keep dashboard data current when project path changes.
If heartbeat-critical work interrupts a task, check afterward and retry the interrupted task if still safe and useful.

items.name: Operation recovery sweep
items.purpose: Reconcile unfinished safe operations until they reach a terminal state.
currentBehavior: Check operations-log.json for pending/running/uncertain work.
Retry safe work only when verification is inconclusive and retry is safe.
Escalate to needs_review instead of looping forever when risk or repeated failure appears.

items.name: Telegram fallback bridge
items.interval: Checked on heartbeat when at least 1 hour has passed since last Telegram check
items.purpose: Catch new human DMs while native messaging remains broken.
currentBehavior: Compare Telegram updates against last handled update id.
Reply briefly via direct Telegram Bot API when a new DM appears.
Close temporary Telegram tabs after use.

items.name: Outlook email watcher
items.interval: Checked on heartbeat when at least 1 hour has passed since last email check, except quiet hours
items.purpose: Surface relevant unread work/business email without babysitting the inbox.
currentBehavior: Unread-only review.
Quiet hours: skip email checks from 11:00 PM to 10:00 AM local time.
Archive-first, moderate handling.
Escalate meaningful ambiguities when human judgment is needed.

items.name: Browser tab maintenance
items.interval: Applied during heartbeat and overnight work
items.purpose: Normalize recurring top-level page tabs so browser state stays clean and predictable.
currentBehavior: Close extra Outlook, GitHub, Telegram API, and other recurring top-level page tabs.
Close blank/new-tab pages when not needed.
Ignore worker/service-worker noise unless debugging.
Prefer a clean working browser state over tab buildup.

items.name: Trista daily morning report
items.purpose: Give Trista direct morning visibility into project progress, active track status, and the dashboard link.
currentBehavior: Send a concise morning report covering active track status, latest meaningful progress, what is being assessed or built now, and immediate next moves.
Prefer replying in the existing known-working Trista thread instead of starting a fresh standalone compose when practical.
Include the dashboard link in the report.
Verify the send because Outlook can be flaky and may leave drafts under poor network conditions.

items: Set Thriving Dancer as a staged product direction and agreed on discovery first, website first, app later.
Established preference for PM/dev/QA style workflow once implementation ramps up.
Defined a stronger mission around building the business before the studio.
Reviewed Trista's LinkedIn and recognized a broader moat than just dance classes.
Built and published the first public Thriving Dancer landing page on GitHub Pages.
Started direct email communication with Trista and sent follow-up discovery questions.
Added autonomous project heartbeat for Thriving Dancer.
Built and published the project dashboard.
Created a working discovery brief from Trista's current signal.
Captured stronger founder answers on audience, launch channel, early products, urgency, and long-term software direction.
Added light passphrase gates to both the dashboard and homepage proof of concept.
Created Planning Pack v1, the website/content architecture draft, the homepage copy draft, and the Start Here hub structure.

Created the about-page outline, tightened hero/CTA options with a recommendation to use a combined Start Here destination first, defined the Start Here hub structure, created a first-visit flow draft, and made the public gates minimal-text only.
Improved heartbeat rules so interrupted tasks can be resumed after heartbeat-critical work finishes and so GitHub-visible updates trigger a message with a link.

Tightened the Thriving Dancer project heartbeat cadence first to every 30 minutes and later to every 15 minutes, created a local Start Here prototype, added a launch sequencing recommendation, and added explicit per-track dashboard status expectations.

projectLog.title: Hardened dashboard blocker and publish-state visibility
projectLog.detail: Updated the local dashboard so blockers live in their own tab, Start Here is linked directly, and public sync / QA state is shown explicitly instead of being inferred.

projectLog.title: Drafted first parent-lane long-form article
projectLog.detail: Created /blog/how-parents-can-support-a-serious-young-dancer-without-over-managing.html and updated the parent lane page so the first parent guidance article is now a real local destination.

projectLog.title: Locked first content-release decision
projectLog.detail: Created  irst-content-release-recommendation.md to make the next publish step explicit: publish the first dancer article and refreshed dashboard feed, then build the first parent article next.

projectLog.title: Drafted first dancer-lane long-form article
projectLog.detail: Created /blog/what-pre-professional-ballet-training-actually-asks-of-you.html and updated the dancer lane page so the first guidance article is now a real local destination.

projectLog.title: Set Thriving Dancer as a staged project
projectLog.detail: Agreed on discovery first, website first, and app/product ideas later.

projectLog.title: Clarified project mission
projectLog.detail: Reframed the work as building the business infrastructure, brand, offers, and systems before a physical studio exists.

projectLog.title: Published first public site
projectLog.detail: Created and deployed the first Thriving Dancer landing page on GitHub Pages.

projectLog.title: Started direct founder communication
projectLog.detail: Began email exchange with Trista and gathered initial bio, mission, and philosophy signal.

projectLog.title: Captured stronger founder answers
projectLog.detail: Confirmed first audience, first launch channel, early products, urgency, and long-term software direction.

projectLog.title: Dashboard and heartbeat became operating surfaces
projectLog.detail: The dashboard went live and the autonomous project heartbeat started driving next-step assessment.

projectLog.title: Dashboard hardening and cleanup
projectLog.detail: Added a light passphrase screen, richer project-log structure, task-finish notification note, and reduced Outlook to one main useful tab.

projectLog.title: Created Planning Pack v1
projectLog.detail: Turned the founder signal into positioning, audience, offer ladder, first launch channels, website structure, and strategic sequence.

projectLog.title: Created website/content architecture draft
projectLog.detail: Translated Planning Pack v1 into homepage goals, messaging hierarchy, page structure, content buckets, and CTA strategy.

projectLog.title: Created homepage copy draft
projectLog.detail: Turned the site architecture into a first pass of page copy for the homepage hero and main sections.

projectLog.title: Created about-page outline
projectLog.detail: Turned the founder story, credibility, philosophy, audience, and CTA into a page-ready narrative outline.

projectLog.title: Created hero and CTA options
projectLog.detail: Tightened the homepage top-of-page language, recommended a combined Start Here path, and set up the next step around a simple content hub.

projectLog.title: Created Start Here hub structure
projectLog.detail: Defined the first-click hub page so the homepage CTA can route dancers and parents into a simple guided content path.

projectLog.title: Created first-visit flow v1
projectLog.detail: Connected the homepage draft, About outline, hero options, and a 3-path Start Here block into a clearer first public visitor journey.

projectLog.title: Created first public launch checklist
projectLog.detail: Defined the lean first-release page set, CTA map, navigation, acceptance checklist, and recommendation to use the blog as the first live content destination.

projectLog.title: Added interruption-retry and GitHub visibility rules
projectLog.detail: Updated heartbeat guidance so interrupted bounded tasks can be retried after heartbeat-critical work and GitHub-visible updates should trigger a concise message with a link.

projectLog.title: Created local Start Here prototype
projectLog.detail: Built a concrete Start Here page prototype to turn routing logic into a reviewable UI and support the next public-facing implementation step.

projectLog.title: Added launch sequencing recommendation
projectLog.detail: Turned the founder signal into a concrete recommendation for homepage -> Start Here -> About -> first blog lanes, with podcast following after the written guidance lanes are established.

projectLog.title: Added explicit per-track dashboard status
projectLog.detail: Defined a clearer dashboard model so each active track exposes its current status, last action, current assessment, next move, and last updated time.

projectLog.title: Scheduled Trista daily morning report
projectLog.detail: Added a daily 10:15 AM America/New_York morning report workflow for Trista so she gets project status and the dashboard link automatically.

projectLog.title: Created first content topics shortlist
projectLog.detail: Turned the vague first-content question into a concrete v1 shortlist of five dancer topics and three parent topics for the earliest written guidance lanes.

projectLog.title: Created publish-ready Start Here page
projectLog.detail: Converted the local prototype into `start-here.html`, a cleaner standalone page intended to become the homepage's first real CTA destination.

projectLog.title: Wired homepage CTA to Start Here locally
projectLog.detail: Updated the local homepage nav and primary CTA so the public-facing path can become homepage -> Start Here instead of dropping visitors into an unrelated section.

projectLog.title: Made Trista thread reuse a durable rule
projectLog.detail: Locked in the rule to use the existing known-working Trista email thread by default and confirmed project records still reflect the 30-minute Thriving Dancer heartbeat cadence.

projectLog.title: Resent Trista update in the working thread
projectLog.detail: Retried the daily/update-style message inside the existing known-working Trista thread; Outlook closed the reply composer cleanly and marked the thread as replied, which was materially better than the earlier failed fresh-compose path.

projectLog.title: Hardened heartbeat significance rule
projectLog.detail: Raised the Thriving Dancer heartbeat quality bar so each run should aim for a meaningful bounded advance, not just passive status confirmation, and added weak-pass visibility expectations to the project records.

projectLog.title: Added recent heartbeat breadcrumbs
projectLog.detail: Added visible recent-heartbeat summaries, quality labels, and last-run metadata to the dashboard feed so the project heartbeat leaves visible evidence instead of disappearing into background execution.

projectLog.title: Started publishing homepage -> Start Here path
projectLog.detail: Staged the local homepage and `start-here.html` for GitHub Pages so the cleaner public visitor flow could move from local-only into the live site.

projectLog.title: Published homepage -> Start Here path
projectLog.detail: Committed the homepage CTA/nav update and the standalone Start Here page to the GitHub Pages repo, then sent Kirill the homepage, Start Here, and dashboard links on Telegram.

projectLog.title: Changed heartbeat cadence to 15 minutes and tightened blocker status visibility
projectLog.detail: Updated the actual Thriving Dancer cron from every 30 minutes to every 15 minutes and changed dashboard blockers to carry explicit status plus communicated-to-Kirill visibility.

projectLog.title: Published dashboard catch-up update
projectLog.detail: Committed the newer dashboard feed and renderer so the public dashboard now reflects the 15-minute cadence, blocker status, communicated-to-Kirill state, and recent-heartbeat breadcrumbs.

projectLog.title: Created CTA destination map v1
projectLog.detail: Turned the first-content shortlist into a concrete destination map for homepage and Start Here CTAs so the next lane-page implementation step is explicit.

projectLog.title: Created first dancer-lane placeholder page
projectLog.detail: Built `/blog/dancers.html` as the first real lane destination and rewired the Start Here dancer CTA toward it locally.

projectLog.title: Created first parent-lane placeholder page
projectLog.detail: Built `/blog/parents.html` as the first real parent-lane destination and rewired the Start Here parent CTA toward it locally.

projectLog.title: Created About/philosophy placeholder page
projectLog.detail: Built `/about.html` as the first real About/philosophy destination and rewired the Start Here third card toward it locally, completing the local three-lane scaffold.

projectLog.title: Published three-lane Start Here scaffold
projectLog.detail: Published the updated Start Here page plus the dancer, parent, and about placeholder pages to GitHub Pages and sent Kirill the public links on Telegram.
